caption:
I spend hours here when I come to Paris. La Grande Épicerie de Paris is a Paris institution that’s been curating the best of the best for over 100 years!

Founded as part of the legendary Le Bon Marché, this gourmet food hall has spent decades sourcing the best products from France and around the world. Now you’ll find everything from mineral rich fleur de sel and traditional ferments to small batch olive oils, medicinal teas, raw honeys, and artisanal foods made with ingredients you can actually recognize.

What I love most is that wellness here isn’t a marketing strategy it’s just tradition (like in Japan). A lot of these products have been staples of French kitchens for generations.

La Grande Épicerie is a nice reminder that health is built on simple things: clean ingredients, whole foods, and time honored traditions 🇫🇷❤️
.
.
.
.
.
#japanese #paris #wellness #travel #health
enriched_note: Gourmet food hall at Le Bon Marché

caption:
What to do with kids during a Paris heatwave??? 

Save this if you have a summer trip to Paris planned!!! 

Paris is in the midst of a pretty toasty period! I know everyone is freaking out based on all the emails piling up in my inbox:) unfortunately Paris is not a city very well adapted to heat… that said the mornings have been cool, and I feel like there is a sense of commanderie throughout the city as everyone suffers together??? 

Here are some tried and true options for a bit of respite from the high temps: 

❄️The Jardin d’Acclimatation - this is a very aesthetically pleasing vintage theme park right outside of Paris. There are some of the only reliable splash pads in the city, along with tons of roller coasters and other activities for all ages. Bonus if you can go on a weekday, especially before the beginning of July when French schools get out, to beat the crowds. Another great aspect - you can decide to go last minute since there are always tix available!

❄️ The Rodin Museum
full_name: Zoë Petit, The Petit Guide - Paris with Kids and Family Travel

caption:
One of my favorite things about living in Paris with kids is knowing the city well enough to pair places together for the perfect outing.
✨

Today we started with absolutely no plans and somehow ended up having one of those perfect Paris days.

We decided to head to Palais Royal, and since Galerie Vivienne and my favorite toy shop, Si Tu Veux, are just nearby, we made a day of it. The teddy bears came along for the adventure, of course. 🧸

What was meant to be a quick outing turned into hours of exploring, wandering, playing, and soaking up the city. We squeezed in a delicious lunch nearby and finished the afternoon with ice cream in the gardens.

Proof that some of the best family days are the ones you don’t plan.

Check my Stories to see where we had lunch! 💛

Discover the magic of Guignol! This iconic puppet theatre has been delighting families in Paris and all across France for over 200 years! Originally from Lyon, Guignol is still a beloved way French parents introduce their kids to theatre from a young age—mixing humor, life lessons, and local culture. 

Kids love it, adults cherish it! The interactive atmosphere (kids are encouraged to shout out and get involved) can keep kids from 2 years old to 10 years old entertained for 30-45 minutes. 

Who else has childhood memories of these timeless puppet shows? Save this reel for your next family visit to France, or drop a comment about your favorite Guignol moment! 

#GuignolTheatre #ParisCulture #FrenchTraditions #paris #kidlife #momlife #frenchlife #parislife
full_name: Kelly-Anne Lyons | American Mom in Paris
enriched_address: Le Théâtre de Guignol, Route de La Muette à Neuilly, Quartier de la Porte-Dauphine, Paris 16e Arrondissement, Paris, Nanterre, Hauts-de-Seine, Île-de-France, France métropolitaine, 92200, France

caption:
One of the most unique installations in Paris right now. 
Known for her iconic fog sculptures, Japanese artist Fujiko Nakaya has transformed the rotunda of the Bourse de Commerce with Cloud #07156. 
Made entirely from water vapour, the installation regularly fills the space beneath the building’s historic dome with mist, causing visitors to appear and disappear as it moves through the rotunda.

📍 Bourse de Commerce, Paris
📅 Until 14 September 2026
🕒 11am–7pm (until 9pm Fridays)

Shot on @sonyalpha @sonyfrance

.
.
.

💌 or DM for shoot enquiries

#parisphotographer #travelphotographer #boursedecommerce fujikonakaya parisart sonyalpha sonyfrance visualstorytelling

Most unique *and* kid-friendly Paris expo of the summer!

Can you imagine stepping into a cloud? That is exactly what 93 year old Japanese artist Fujiko Nakaya creates with one of her “Fog Sculptures”, titled “Cloud #07156,” which has overtaken the rotonde at the Bourse de Commerce museum.  This is exactly what I love in a museum@show for kids - hands on, interactive, and something that makes you discuss afterwards. The goal is to get kids feeling comfortable and dare I say excited to go to a museum:) 

We visited over the weekend and it was a huge hit with all the kids in the space! Kids (and adults:)) jumped and danced through the cloud, trying to guess which spot would spout out the cloud next. We left all dewy with water droplets on eyelashes and hair from the cloud. It was entertaining and magical all at once! Thank you for the invite, @boursedecommerce 💕

On til sept 14. Open Monday to Sunday from 11 a.m. to 7 p.m.
Late opening on Fridays until 9 p.m.
Closed on Tuesdays and on Ma

caption:
You’ll seriously regret skipping this spot on your next trip to Paris! 🏛️☕️✨

Right in the heart of the city, just steps away from the chaotic crowds of the Champs-Élysées, lies a serene oasis that most tourists walk right past: Café 1902, located inside the stunning Petit Palais. 🌿

This isn’t just a coffee break; it’s a full Belle Époque dream. Surrounded by exotic palm trees, golden mosaics, and grand colonnades, it’s easily one of the most aesthetic spots in the entire city.

The best part? Access to the museum’s permanent collection and this incredible inner courtyard is completely FREE. 🤫🏛️ It’s the ultimate « main character » location without the price tag.

Put this at the very top of your Paris bucket list before everyone else finds out! ✨🥐

📍 Location: Avenue Winston Churchill, 75008 Paris (Inside Petit Palais)

#ParisSecrets #PetitPalais #pariscafe #pariscoffee #coffeeshop

I was very lucky to see the bridge covered with wisteria 💜 as well as cherry blossoms 🌸 in full bloom in the park today!
I can’t believe it took me so long to come visit it!

caption:
There’s a park in Paris most people never find… and for me, it’s the most beautiful in the city.

Parc Monceau feels completely different — less structured, more romantic, almost like a hidden garden.

Designed as a kind of fantasy landscape, it blends real historic fragments with more whimsical architectural details… which is what makes it feel so unique.

If you’re coming to Paris and want a slower, more relaxed afternoon, this is where to go.

📍 Parc Monceau — 35 Boulevard de Courcelles, 75008 Paris

Save this for your next trip ✨

caption:
Ever since I posted my last reel about the light show at Saint-Eustache Church, I’ve received so many messages from people wanting to experience it too… only to find out the show had already ended.

So I went to test another immersive light experience in Paris for you — and this one is completely different.

This time, it takes place inside the breathtaking Dôme des Invalides at Les Invalides. Unlike Saint-Eustache, there’s no live choir here — instead, AURA Invalides combines monumental projection mapping, music, lights, and an immersive self-guided visit through the monument itself. ✨

The experience lasts around 50 minutes and includes a 15-minute intermission where you can freely explore the chapels and hidden spaces of the Invalides before the second part of the show begins. And honestly… seeing the golden dome illuminated with music echoing through the building feels completely surreal.

You’re not just sitting and watching a show — you’re walking through centuries of history whi

The most private garden in Montmartre is hidden behind an iron gate most people walk past without noticing.

L’Hôtel Particulier feels less like a hotel and more like a secret Paris mansion frozen behind trees, gravel paths, and quiet stone walls. Once the gate opens, the noise of the city disappears almost instantly.

Inside, the garden stays intentionally understated. Mature trees, overgrown hedges, weathered gravel, and pale limestone facades create a space that feels completely detached from the streets outside.

Best time:
Late afternoon or overcast mornings

What to expect:
A hidden private mansion garden
Quiet dining beneath old trees
A reservation-only atmosphere

Do’s:
Reserve in advance
Walk slowly through the courtyard
Notice the smaller architectural details

Don’ts:
Don’t expect a traditional hotel entrance
Don’t arrive without a reservation
Don’t rush the garden experience

Some places in Paris still feel genuinely hidden 🇫🇷.

#Paris #Montmartre #HiddenParis #ParisTravel

caption:
Comment PARIS for my map to the city! 

The prettiest paper store in Paris, and the perfect souvenir for those of us who like something unexpected and beautiful! 

If you’re planning a trip to Paris and love antiques and design, you’re going to want to save this post and make sure to visit…@MelodiesGraphiques. 

There’s just something about beautiful paper, the feeling of it, the weight, the intention…if you know, you know.

As a designer living here, I love stopping in to pick up stationary for our client presentations.

I stumbled into this shop while walking home from lunch one day, it reminded me of some of the things my friend Sarah has in her home. It’s simply darling and is located right near the Seine near one of my favorite cafes (Chez Julien) just outside the Marais. 

You’ll find stationary, calligraphy supplies, wrapping paper, stamps, and even wax seals. Some of the beautiful 3D cards reminded me of the beautiful things @chateausonoma has in her house and shop!

How specia

caption:
Some of the most magical and romantic places in Paris were spots I didn’t see on many travel guides or “must visit” lists… so I’m adding them to mine! ✨☕️ 

If you were my bff heading to Paris, here are 7 spots I’d tell you not to miss:
1️⃣ Musée Rodin: Sunlit rooms and romantic gardens filled with famous Rodin sculptures
2️⃣ Maison Fleuret: A book lovers dream cafe
3️⃣ Jardin du Palais Royal: A tree-lined park filled with fountains and cute cafe patios
4️⃣ Au Facteur Cheval: The most magical antique store outside Versailles
5️⃣ Cite de l’Architecture et du Patrimoine: Grand French architecture mixed with breathtaking views of the Eiffel Tower
6️⃣ Saint Ouen Flea Market: The ultimate Parisian treasure hunting
7️⃣ Hôtel du Quai Voltaire: A cozy hotel with breathtaking sunrise views over the Seine

🗺️ Want my full 7-Day Paris Itinerary with all of these stops + more? Comment LINK and I’ll DM it to you! 

#travelguide #paris #parisguide #parisitinerary #paristips | travel guide, favorite

caption:
Looking for an easy and FUN Paris museum to visit?

We visited the somewhat-new-to-the-Paris-scene Philharmonie des Enfants @philharmoniedeparis and all agreed it was excellent. If you are a music lover or just want a lighthearted and uncrowded museum - this is for you!!! It had everything I look for in a successful museum experience for kids, including tons of hands on activities and all text translated in English. We played pong with our voices, learned about music from all over the world, added sound to silent movies, learned about how sound is transmitted, formed a family band and mixed beats… it was so fun and we ALL loved it!

Pair this museum with lunch at the Boom Boom Villette food court, searching for the 7 new space invaders on the Philharmonie building (see my previous reel!) and a spin at the dragon slide in the Parc de la Villette!

We reserved our 1 hour and 15 mins time slot the morning of, and arrived 15 mins early. 

Recommended for kids 4-10; ideally reserve your tim

caption:
🇫🇷 We didn’t expect to find a peaceful Eiffel Tower view in Paris, but this little architecture museum surprised me. Most people rush past it on their way to Trocadéro without even noticing. We walked in with zero queue, wandered through the quiet hall, and ended up standing in front of a window perfectly framing the tower. 

And if you come in the winter months when the sun sets early, you can watch the Eiffel Tower sparkling from inside ✨

⏰ The museum is open from 11 am to 7 pm, except on Thursdays, when it stays open until 9 pm (great for night views)

💰Admission: 13 €; free entry: first Sunday of each month

📍Cité de l’architecture et du patrimoine, Paris

Don’t skip this in Paris 😍⬇️

📍 Café – Restaurant Le Jardin du Petit Palais

Follow @helloangelia for more hidden gems in Paris 🇫🇷✨

One of our favorite quiet spots in Paris is hiding inside the Petit Palais museum. The café sits in a beautiful garden courtyard with a fountain and museum views, and it feels like a little break from the city 🌿

And the best part is that the museum is free. It’s perfect place for a casual lunch, coffee break, or just to relax🍴

Save this for your Paris list 🤍 and sends this to someone you want to go here with ✨

caption:
Places we love exploring in Paris with kids:
1. RORI @rorirorirorirorirori 
Marcel loves this place : the Red Pie is his favorite slice, and we often stop here for a quick pizza break.
2. JARDIN DES TUILERIES
The perfect place for kids to jump on the trampolines and run around.
3. GRANDE GALERIE DE L’ÉVOLUTION
An iconic place where history and science come together to tell the story of biodiversity and kids are always fascinated by the animals.
4. LE FAVORI @lefavoriparis 
The restaurant run by Marcel and Michel’s dad and obviously our favorite place for lunch.
5. AQUARIUM TROPICAL DE LA PORTE DORÉE
A beautiful aquarium inside a stunning Art Deco building.
6. SEINE CRUISE @vedettesdupontneuf 
A magical way to see Paris from the river. Kids love spotting the monuments from the boat.
7. RAIMO @raimoglacier 
Raimo is one of the oldest ice cream shops in Paris, and one of the best.
8. GRANDE MOSQUÉE DE PARIS
Mint tea and pastries in the peaceful courtyard : a little escape in the middle of
enriched_address: RORI, 96, Rue Jean-Pierre Timbaud, Quartier de la Folie-Méricourt, Paris 11e Arrondissement, Paris, Île-de-France, France métropolitaine, 75011, France

caption:
Sharing one of the best, off-the-beaten-path playgrounds in Paris today! 

➡️Save this for your next Paris vacation! 

The “Folies / Jardins des vents et des dunes” (Garden of Wind and Dunes) playground is SO unique - I guarantee you’ve never seen all this playground equipment before:) 

Faves are the ‘trampolines’ (using that term loosely:)), zip lines, hamster 🐹 wheels (yep 😂), tipis, and stationary bike windmills! I told you it was unique! There are also tons of lounge chairs for maximum parental relaxation 👌. 

The playground is gated (read : relaxing for parents) and you go through a red building to enter. There is a security guard at the entrance, and an enclosed stroller storage area. 

Beware - This playground can fill up, so try to come early in the morning or late in the afternoon. 

The best part - it’s FREE!

You can find this playground in the huge Parc de la Villette in the 19th arrondissement, near the northwestern corner of the Grande Halle de la Villette building. It’s

If you’re planning a Paris trip this summer, Airelles Gordes is the perfect add on destination and only 2.5 hours by train. 🌿

(Also a great bonus spot before or after St Tropez!)

It sits above one of the most beautiful villages in Provence, and yes, this is one of the restaurants Emily in Paris was filmed. 😉 

My kids were a little young for the excursions, so we mostly did kids club and pool time. But for families with older kids who love adventure this place would be incredible. There are so many activities the concierge can organize from: hiking, biking, horse back riding, rope tree courses, karting, kayaking, off roading, rock climbing, paragliding, and village exploring.

As always, I’m here to answer any questions about our experience and logistics! 🤍

caption:
Just 90 minutes from Paris, Les Bordes Estate in the Loire Valley blends luxury, community, and nature. Home to a world-renowned private golf club, equestrian centre, endless outdoor pursuits, and a future Six Senses Resort & Spa, the estate is designed for well-being and balance.

Now available: twelve fully furnished Six Senses residences (3–7 bedrooms, 210–662 m²) with heated pools, south-facing gardens, and full resort access—including a Michelin-level restaurant, 18,000 ft² spa, and wellness facilities. At the heart of the estate are two acclaimed championship golf courses, while the equestrian centre, led by Olympic gold medallist Scott Brash, offers elite riding experiences.

Set to open in 2026, these turnkey residences combine timeless design with world-class, fully serviced living.

caption:
📍The Venice of Périgord is the second name of Brantôme, a dreamy small town in the southwest of France (about 2 hours from Bordeaux). 

The city center is a charming island surrounded by the Dronne River. You can circle it by boat or canoe, or walk around, stopping at numerous cafés, art galleries, and antique shops.

Périgord itself is a magnificent region that’s absolutely worth a visit. It’s also the world capital of black truffle (an AOP product originating from Périgord, meaning true black truffles only grow here!)

Beyond Brantôme en Périgord, there are many amazing bucket list places to explore in the region:

📍The towns of Périgueux and Sarlat-la-Canéda,
📍The Lascaux Caves (home to the world’s most famous prehistoric drawings),
📍Les Jardins de Marqueyssac (some of France’s most famous and picturesque gardens),
📍The truffle markets and festivals (held during truffle season from November to February),
📍The numerous châteaux and vineyards of Bergerac

To me, this is probably the m

📌 Save this for when you visit Paris 🇫🇷✨

📍Café - Restaurant Le Jardin du Petit Palais

Located in the heart of my favorite museum, Petit Palais, this cafe is a hidden gem that you can’t miss! When you visit, I highly recommend sitting on the terrace and soaking up the beauty of the museum, fountain, and garden while having a delicious lunch. Petit Palais is free to enter, so you can visit this cafe for free as well! 🍴
